page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized virtual machine featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon processor with 12 vCPUs and 6 physical cores. Operating at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz, the x86_64 CPU architecture includes 38 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. The system is equipped with 85 GB of system memory, providing approximately 7.08 GB of RAM per core. Crucially, this instance bundles a single NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 40 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not included in this configuration, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear divergence between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25%, reflecting the moderate 2.2 GHz clock speed. Multi-core CPU execution and memory bandwidth tests, including large-block uncached reads and writes, perform in the middle 50% tier. However, the integrated NVIDIA A100 GPU delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference tasks. Specifically, prompt processing and text generation for small (135M) and medium (7B) parameter models achieve highly efficient throughput, though prompt processing for larger 70B models drops to average levels.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the a2-highgpu-1g represents a specialized entry point into GPU-accelerated computing. While the CPU resources and memory bandwidth are moderate, the inclusion of a high-end enterprise GPU makes it highly cost-effective for workloads that offload computation to the accelerator rather than relying on raw CPU power. This server is optimized for machine learning workloads, including LLM inference, deep learning training, and data science pipelines. It is less suitable for CPU-heavy compilation, single-threaded legacy applications, or high-capacity local storage databases due to the lack of bundled local drives and lower single-core CPU performance.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for GPU-intensive workloads. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon CPU with 12 vCPUs, 6 physical cores, and a 2.2 GHz clock speed, paired with 85 GB of system memory. The primary hardware highlight is a bundled NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 40 GB of VRAM. While single-core CPU benchmarks place this instance in the bottom 25% and multi-core performance sits in the middle 50%, its GPU capabilities deliver top-tier performance for large language model inference, particularly with 135M and 7B parameter models. Local storage is not included. This instance is highly cost-effective for machine learning, deep learning, and data science tasks that offload processing to the GPU, though it is less suited for CPU-bound or storage-heavy workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 85 GB of RAM, and a bundled NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 40 GB of VRAM for top-tier AI inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g features 12 vCPUs, 85 GB RAM, and a bundled NVIDIA A100 GPU, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized instance pairing an NVIDIA A100 GPU with dedicated Intel Xeon processors for high-throughput machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU featuring 40 GB of VRAM.
Features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 85 GB of system memory.
Delivers top-tier performance for 135M and 7B parameter LLM inference.
No local storage is bundled with this instance configuration.
Best suited for machine learning, deep learning, and data science workloads.
